name: Gna
description: Gna is a goddess in Norse mythology associated with messages and errands. She serves the goddess Frigg as a messenger, riding the horse Hófvarpnir ('hoof-thrower') through the air and over sea. Gna is mentioned in the Prose Edda books Gylfaginning and Skáldskaparmál, written in the 13th century by Snorri Sturluson. Snorri describes her as one of Frigg's handmaidens and says that Frigg sends her on errands to various worlds. Her horse, Hófvarpnir, is capable of traveling through the air and over the sea, making Gna a swift and efficient messenger. Gna is also said to possess knowledge of events happening in different realms, which she reports back to Frigg. While not a major deity with extensive myths, Gna embodies the role of a loyal and capable servant, highlighting the importance of communication and information gathering within the Norse cosmos. Her swiftness and ability to traverse boundaries underscore the interconnectedness of the various realms in Norse cosmology. Gna's existence also demonstrates the presence of lesser deities and beings who play crucial supporting roles in the grand narrative of Norse mythology, facilitating the actions of the more prominent gods and goddesses. She represents diligence, speed, and the crucial function of relaying information in a world where knowledge is power. Her name may be related to the Old Norse verb 'gná', meaning 'to cause to rub' or 'to scrape', possibly alluding to the swift movement of her horse or the gathering of information.
subtitle: Frigg's Swift Messenger and Errand-Runner

keyMyths.title: Gna's Journeys
keyMyths.description: Gna is sent by Frigg on various errands throughout the nine realms, gathering information and delivering messages. Her swift horse, Hófvarpnir, allows her to travel with incredible speed.

keyMyths.title: Hófvarpnir's Speed
keyMyths.description: Hófvarpnir is described as being able to run through the air and over the sea, highlighting the extraordinary speed and capability of Gna's mount. This allows Gna to fulfill her duties effectively.

keyMyths.title: Reporting to Frigg
keyMyths.description: Gna returns to Frigg with the information she gathers, sharing her knowledge of events happening in different realms. This makes her a valuable asset to Frigg's understanding of the cosmos.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: The primary source of information about Gna, detailing her role as Frigg's messenger.
